name: dev.ucp.common.identity_linking
title: Identity Linking
description: Capability schema for identity linking. Businesses declare trusted identity providers and per-capability scope requirements in the capability config.
$comment: Scope declarations live in config.capabilities, not on individual capability schemas. Each entry declares what scopes the business offers for buyer-authenticated access and whether buyer auth is required. Platforms derive the authorization scope set by collecting scopes from this map, intersected with the negotiated capabilities.

capability_identity_config.description: Per-capability identity configuration. Declares whether buyer auth is required and what scopes govern buyer-scoped access. When scopes is absent, the capability name itself is the scope (coarse-grained). When scopes is present, each entry is appended to the capability name to form the wire-format scope token.

required.description: Whether this capability requires buyer identity (obtained via identity linking). When true, the business returns an identity_required error for requests without a buyer identity token. When false (default), the capability works at agent-auth level and buyer identity upgrades the experience.

scopes.description: Sub-scope identifiers within the capability (e.g., 'read', 'manage'). Defined by the capability's own spec as operation groups. Each entry is joined to the capability name with a colon on the wire (e.g., 'dev.ucp.shopping.order:read'). When absent, the capability name itself is the scope.

provider.description: An identity provider that authenticates buyers via OAuth 2.0.

auth_url.description: Base URL for authorization server metadata discovery. Platforms resolve metadata via RFC 8414 (/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server) with OIDC fallback (/.well-known/openid-configuration) on 404.

business_schema.title: Identity Linking (Business)
business_schema.description: Business-level identity linking configuration declaring trusted identity providers and per-capability scope requirements.

providers.description: Map of trusted identity providers keyed by reverse-domain identifier.

capabilities.description: Map of capabilities that offer buyer-scoped features, keyed by capability name. Capabilities absent from this map have no buyer-scoped features. Platforms derive the authorization scope set from this map intersected with the negotiated capabilities.
